name: Puma
description: Puma is a high-performance, multi-threaded web server for Ruby applications. It is designed for concurrency, using a threading model combined with a reactor pattern to handle multiple requests simultaneously with minimal resource consumption.
advantages: Lower memory usage per request compared to Unicorn
Works with all major Ruby web frameworks
Supports both thread and process modes
disadvantages: Thread safety requires careful gem selection
Debugging concurrent issues can be challenging
Not as performant as compiled language servers
Configuration can be complex for optimal tuning
